creator: jeff+freebsd@wagsky.com
text:
Definitely "interesting", though I'm not sure yet what to make of it.
.101 is the one that needs to be up for `pkg update` to work.

Changing /etc/resolv.conf to 

nameserver	9.9.9.11
#nameserver     10.2.53.102                                                                                            
#nameserver     10.2.53.101   

did not resolve the problem (still freezes). 
Rebooting after that change did not resolve the problem.

=============================================================
I'm going to re-examine when I can let the caches expire.
I've also got a third nameserver up and will check with that.
=============================================================



#!/bin/sh

drill @10.2.53.101 _http._tcp.pkg.freebsd.org srv > 101.out
drill @10.2.53.102 _http._tcp.pkg.freebsd.org srv > 102.out
drill @9.9.9.11 _http._tcp.pkg.freebsd.org srv > ext.out

drill -D @10.2.53.101 _http._tcp.pkg.freebsd.org srv > 101.D.out
drill -D @10.2.53.102 _http._tcp.pkg.freebsd.org srv > 102.D.out
drill -D @9.9.9.11 _http._tcp.pkg.freebsd.org srv > ext.D.out


The two servers seem to differ mainly (only?) in that .101 is returning IPv6
results as well.

[jeff@js-apu ~/bug-254497/2022-04-30]$ cat both-up/101.out 
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, rcode: NOERROR, id: 20347
;; flags: qr rd ra ; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 6, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 8 
;; QUESTION SECTION:

;; ANSWER SECTION:

_http._tcp.pkg.freebsd.org.	300	IN	SRV	50 10 80 pkg0.nyi.freebsd.org.

;; AUTHORITY SECTION:

;; ADDITIONAL SECTION:
